title: Fresh rebuild: Top 100 crypto tracker with filters and 12-month price projections

requirements.text: Replace all existing dashboard components with a single clean Top100Dashboard component. Fetch the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market cap from the CoinGecko public API (endpoint: /api/v3/coins/markets, params: vs_currency=usd, order=market_cap_desc, per_page=100, page=1, sparkline=false). Display results in a paginated, sortable table with columns: Rank, Name/Symbol (with coin logo), Price (USD), 24h Change (%), Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio (24h volume ÷ market cap × 100, as a percentage), and Fully Diluted Market Cap. Update App.tsx to render only the new Top100Dashboard.

quotes: tracks the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market cap

acceptanceCriteria: Table renders exactly 100 rows of live CoinGecko data on load
All 8 columns are present and correctly populated
Buy Ratio column shows (volume/marketCap)*100 formatted as a percentage
Table is sortable by clicking any column header
Table is paginated (e.g. 25 rows per page) with page navigation controls
Coin logo thumbnails are displayed alongside name and symbol
All previous dashboard components (Top150Dashboard, MultiCryptoDashboard, etc.) are removed from the rendered page

requirements.text: Add a filter and sort control bar above the Top 100 table. Include: a text search input to filter coins by name or symbol; four dropdown selectors to sort/filter by Live Market Cap, 24h Volume, Buy Ratio, and Fully Diluted Market Cap (each with High→Low and Low→High options); and a Reset button that clears all active filters and search input, restoring the default market-cap-desc order.

quotes: Add specific dropdown tools to filter each cryptocurrency to track live market cap, volume, buy ratio, and fully diluted market cap

acceptanceCriteria: Search input filters the table rows in real time by coin name or ticker symbol (case-insensitive)
Each of the four metric dropdowns correctly reorders the visible rows when a sort direction is selected
Only one sort criterion is active at a time; selecting a new dropdown deselects the previous one
Reset button restores the default sort order and clears the search field
Filter bar is fully visible and usable at 375 px mobile width

requirements.text: Add a dedicated Price Projections section below the Top 100 table. The section must include: a searchable coin selector dropdown populated from the 100 fetched coins; a 12-month rolling projection panel displayed once a coin is selected, showing a monthly table (Month 1–12 from today) with low, mid, and high projected prices and a confidence score per month; and a line chart (using Recharts) visualising the mid projected price trajectory across all 12 months. Projections must be computed entirely on the frontend using the selected coin's current price, 30-day historical price data fetched from CoinGecko (/api/v3/coins/{id}/market_chart?vs_currency=usd&days=30), and linear regression + volatility logic (reuse or adapt frontend/src/utils/projections.ts).

quotes: provide a separate section to filter each cryptocurrencies price projections on a current, 12-month running schedule

acceptanceCriteria: Coin selector dropdown is searchable and lists all 100 coins
Selecting a coin triggers a CoinGecko 30-day history fetch and displays the projection panel
Projection table shows 12 rows (one per month) with low, mid, high prices and a confidence score
Confidence score decreases gradually for later months to reflect growing uncertainty
Line chart renders mid-price trajectory across 12 months with month labels on the x-axis
Loading and error states are handled gracefully with user-facing messages
No external AI API calls are made; all projection logic is algorithmic and runs in the browser

requirements.text: Implement a clean React Query data-fetching layer in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts. Provide a useTopCoins hook (fetches the top 100 coins market data with a 60-second stale time and in-memory cache) and a useCoinHistory hook (fetches 30-day price history for a given coin ID, with a 5-minute stale time). Both hooks must handle loading, error, and empty states, fall back to cached data on API errors, and include rate-limit-safe retry logic (exponential backoff, max 3 retries).

quotes: latest AI and modern software tools

acceptanceCriteria: useTopCoins returns { data, isLoading, isError } and caches results for 60 seconds
useCoinHistory accepts a coin ID string and returns cached 30-day price arrays
On API error, both hooks return the last successful cached value if available
Retry logic uses exponential backoff and does not exceed 3 attempts
No duplicate API calls fire when multiple components consume the same hook simultaneously

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive dark-mode-first visual theme across the entire rebuilt application. Use a near-black charcoal base (#0d0f14 or equivalent), neon cyan primary accent, and gold secondary accent. Use a clean sans-serif typeface. The sticky header must display 'Crypto Tracker AI' branding with a live indicator badge. Tables, cards, and dropdowns must use subtle glassmorphism / frosted-panel styling. Positive 24h price changes render in emerald green, negative in red. The full layout must be responsive down to 375 px mobile width.

quotes: latest AI and modern software tools

acceptanceCriteria: Background is near-black charcoal; neon cyan and gold are used for accents and highlights
Header is sticky and displays 'Crypto Tracker AI' with a pulsing live badge
Table rows, filter cards, and dropdowns have a frosted/glassmorphism appearance
Positive percentage changes are rendered in emerald green, negative in red
Layout renders correctly and is usable at 375 px viewport width with no horizontal scroll
Dark mode is the default; no light-mode toggle is required

constraints: All data fetching must use the public CoinGecko API only — no API key required
No external AI API calls for projections; all forecast logic must run client-side
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Backend (main.mo) must not be changed unless explicitly needed for new state
All empty legacy component files (ICPDashboard, MultiCryptoDashboard, Top50Dashboard, etc.) should be cleaned up or left empty — do not resurrect them
nonGoals: Internet Identity login or user authentication
Saving favourite coins or user preferences to the backend
Portfolio simulation or investment planning features
More than 100 coins in the main table
